title: Investigative Documentary
subtitle: Defend a claim cluster across testimony, evidence, footage, and counter-voices.
scope: An investigative documentary's claim cluster being driven from raw research to a defensible, lockable state.
sourceCoverage: Template only; replace placeholders with real claims, sources, findings, and risks as the work progresses.

templateMeta.useWhen: Use this when an investigative documentary has a claim cluster that needs to stay defensible across testimony, documents, footage, counter-voices, and legal risk — typically mid-production through picture-lock.
templateMeta.operatingThesis: Treat each claim as a lifecycle, not a single check. Defensibility climbs through testimony, corroboration, deliberate counter-voice inclusion, and legal review before reaching the cut.
keepDistinct: the claim and its lifecycle state

objective: Drive each claim in the cluster from untested toward locked-in-cut, surfacing evidence gaps, counter-voice gaps, and legal risk along the way.
successCondition: A reader can say, for each claim, where it sits in the defensibility lifecycle, what would close the next gap, and which legal flags remain open.

objectiveFacets.name: Defensibility lifecycle
objectiveFacets.description: Each claim moves from untested toward locked-in-cut through observable evidence.

objectiveFacets.name: Evidence coverage
objectiveFacets.description: On-camera testimony, documents, and footage converge per claim.

objectiveFacets.name: Counter-voice discipline
objectiveFacets.description: Opposing testimony is deliberately included, not omitted.

objectiveFacets.name: Legal-risk read
objectiveFacets.description: AETA, libel, and defamation exposure is tracked per claim before lock.

objectiveFacets.name: Proof staging
objectiveFacets.description: Logic-of-proof rungs escalate per claim, not flat checks.

objectiveFacets.name: Continuity through-line
objectiveFacets.description: Personal frame and narrative spine survive across the locked claims.

objectiveFacets.name: Source accumulation
objectiveFacets.description: Dated sources are attributed to the claims they affect.

invariants.name: Claim has defensibility status
invariants.statement: Every card in the claim-defensibility cross-cut carries a defensibility lifecycle status.

invariants.name: Lock requires counter-voice
invariants.statement: A claim cannot move to locked-in-cut without at least one deliberately-included counter-voice on record.

invariants.name: Proof rungs escalate
invariants.statement: Proof-ladder rungs are ordered weakest to strongest; status of a higher rung does not exceed the strongest satisfied lower rung.

callouts.title: How to use this template
items: Scope the doc to one cluster of claims serving one objective (a single act, an episode, a screening).
Let claim-defensibility be the spine; the other sections complement it from different angles.
Retire the doc when the objective is answered — start a new one for the next.

callout.title: Read order
items: Use the snapshot to see where the cluster sits at a glance.
Drive each claim forward through the sections below until the objective is answered.

data.name: Claim placeholder
data.claim: State the proposition the film is advancing.
data.personalFrameLink: How does the filmmaker's voice bridge this claim to the spine?
legalRiskNotes: Read for AETA / libel / defamation exposure.
openGaps: What evidence would advance the lifecycle.
notes: One card per claim. Keep the proposition singular.

data.name: Proof rung placeholder
notes: Each rung is a stronger proof of the claim. Lower rungs first.

data.name: Finding placeholder
notes: Findings are observed facts across the investigation, not decisions.

data.name: Continuity risk placeholder
data.riskSummary: Where the narrative through-line is at risk.
evidence: Evidence and contradictions noted so far.

data.name: Citation placeholder
notes: Each card is one dated source attributed to the claims it affects.
